message:
fix(eval): build ordered pack list and keyword index after external packs are loaded

Previously, ordered_packs and keyword_index were computed before external
packs were loaded from custom_paths, which meant external pack IDs were
excluded from the evaluation iteration list. Commands matching only
external pack keywords would be silently missed by the keyword index
fast-path, causing incorrect allow/deny decisions.

Changes in both main.rs (runtime path) and cli.rs (test-command path):

1. Move ordered_packs and keyword_index construction to after the
   external pack loading and keyword collection phase.

2. Append external pack IDs to ordered_packs since
   expand_enabled_ordered only knows about built-in registry packs and
   would not include externally loaded ones.

3. When external packs are present, disable the keyword_index entirely
   (set to None) to force the non-indexed evaluation path. The keyword
   index only covers built-in packs; using it with external packs would
   cause false negatives where external pack rules are skipped. The
   non-indexed path correctly iterates all packs including external ones.

This ensures external custom packs participate fully in command
evaluation regardless of whether the fast keyword-index path or the
exhaustive iteration path is used.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
